channel.title: NYT > World > Middle East
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay, Eric Schmitt, Qasim Nauman and Ephrat Livni

item.title: This is what happened on March 10.
Iran’s New Supreme Leader Was Wounded Early in the War, Iranian and Israeli Officials Say
item.description: Officials say Mojtaba Khamenei’s legs were hurt, but the circumstances as well as the extent of his injuries were unclear. He has remained out of view since being announced as leader three days ago.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi and Ronen Bergman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A rally in support of Iran’s new leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, in Tehran on Monday.

item.title: How Trump and His Advisers Miscalculated Iran’s Response to War
item.description: In the lead-up to the U.S.-Israeli attack, President Trump downplayed the risks to the energy markets as a short-term concern that should not overshadow the mission to decapitate the Iranian regime.
item.dc:creator: Mark Mazzetti, Tyler Pager and Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Benoit Tessier/Reuters
item.media:description: In response to Iranian threats, commercial shipping has come to a standstill in the Gulf, and oil prices have spiked.

item.title: Missiles, Drones and Airstrikes Pound Middle East Into 12th Day of War
item.description: The effects of the war are being felt through the Persian Gulf.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Fadel Itani/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: An airstrike in Beirut’s southern suburbs late Tuesday night.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Tuesday
item.description: Trump administration officials cast the president as the sole arbiter on the U.S. war effort. International aid groups were warning of a growing humanitarian crisis in Lebanon, where nearly 700,000 people had been displaced, the U.N. said.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: From left, Gen. Dan Caine, the chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Vice President JD Vance at Dover Air Force Base in Delaware on Monday.

item.title: U.S. Forces Attack Iranian Mine-Laying Ships Near the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A video posted by the U.S. Central Command showed munitions striking nine vessels, most of which were moored at the time. Whether any mines have been laid in Gulf waters was unclear.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Amr Alfiky/Reuters
item.media:description: Boats docked in a fishing area in the Strait of Hormuz in Musandam, Oman, earlier this month.

item.title: Democrats Demand Hegseth and Rubio Testify on Iran War
item.description: Senate Democratic leaders called on President Trump to dispatch the senior cabinet officials to make the case to Congress and the American public for the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro and Robert Jimison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Secretary of State Marco Rubio at the Capitol in January.

item.title: Iranian Military Shows It Knows How to Adapt, U.S. Officials Say
item.description: Iran appears to be targeting what it views as American vulnerabilities, including air defenses meant to guard troops and assets in the region.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A new billboard in Tehran on Tuesday illustrating the succession of Iran’s three supreme leaders.

item.title: Trump Tries to Sidestep Blame for Any Civilian Deaths in Iran
item.description: The president and the Pentagon have cast blame on Iran for the mounting toll. More than 1,800 people have died in the war, including many civilians.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump has repeatedly suggested a strike on a girls’ school that reportedly killed 175 people was carried out by Iran or another country.

item.title: War in Iran Could Lead to Food Shortages in Region, Experts Warn
item.description: Some residents of Lebanon, Gaza and Iran are reporting shortages of food, rising food prices and other disruptions to food supplies as the conflict in the Middle East continues.
item.dc:creator: Raja Abdulrahim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: A food distribution center for internally displaced people in Dahiya, the densely populated southern suburb of Beirut that is a stronghold of Hezbollah, last week.

item.title: U.S. Showers Iran With Bombs in Most Intense Strikes of the War, Pentagon Says
item.description: Iranians cowered under the barrage as Pete Hegseth, the U.S. defense secretary, said the U.S. aimed to wipe out Iran’s capacity to obtain nuclear weapons “forever.”
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A new billboard in Valiasr Square in Tehran on Tuesday shows the history of Iran’s three supreme leaders: Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini, his successor Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, and his son Mojtaba Khamenei, who has been chosen as the new supreme leader.

item.title: Imprisoned in Iran, and Under Bombardment
item.description: Iranian detention centers, some holding people swept up in a government crackdown on protests along with other prisoners, have been damaged in the U.S.-Israeli airstrikes.
item.dc:creator: Parin Behrooz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: The remnants of a police station on Niloufar Street in Tehran after it was hit by airstrikes this month.

item.title: Pentagon Says 140 Service Members Have Been Injured in Iran War
item.description: Eight Americans have been seriously wounded, military officials said, but the bulk of the injured have already returned to duty. Seven Americans have been killed.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: A vehicle containing the remains of Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington at Dover Air Force Base in Delaware. He was the seventh American service member killed in the Iran war.

item.title: How Long Will the Iran War Last? Trump Offers Conflicting Answers.
item.description: Now 11 days into an expanding military campaign, President Trump and his officials have given conflicting indications on how long the United States intends the war to last.
item.dc:creator: Aishvarya Kavi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump has left wide-open the possibilities for how the war with Iran could wind down and how long it will take.

item.title: France Bolsters Efforts to Repatriate Citizens Stranded by Iran War
item.description: France is highlighting its repatriation operations for nationals left stranded by the conflict with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Catherine Porter
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Abdul Saboor/Reuters
item.media:description: President Emmanuel Macron of France has refused to join the American and Israeli strikes on Iran.

item.title: 4 Continents in 62 Hours: How One American Got Out of Qatar
item.description: When conflict broke out in the Middle East, thousands of travelers were stranded. We followed a New Orleans doctor on his epic journey to get home.
item.dc:creator: Christine Chung
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Nearly 700,000 Displaced by War in Lebanon, U.N. Says
item.description: Mass evacuation orders and an intensifying Israeli bombing campaign targeting the Iran-backed militia Hezbollah have caused a humanitarian crisis, aid groups warn.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Smoke rises from an Israeli airstrike in Beirut, Lebanon, on Tuesday.

item.title: ‘Nothing Will Remain of Tehran,’ Iranians Say Amid Heavy Bombing
item.description: Some residents described the strikes overnight Monday into Tuesday as among the worst they had experienced since the war started.
item.dc:creator: Leily Nikounazar and Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Iran has faced ongoing bombing from the U.S. and Israel.

item.title: U.S. Eases Limits on Russian Energy as Oil Prices Soar
item.description: The volatility in energy markets because of the war in Iran could not come at a better time for President Vladimir V. Putin.
item.dc:creator: Paul Sonne
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Alexey Malgavko/Reuters
item.media:description: An oil refinery in Omsk, Russia, in 2023.

item.title: Higher Oil Prices Could Put the Fed in a Bind as Labor Market Softens
item.description: Investors now expect that the Fed will delay a rate cut until September instead of July, as they had before the war in Iran began.
item.dc:creator: Colby Smith
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Ken Cedeno/Reuters
item.media:description: Economists expect energy-related price pressures to push inflation up to some degree in the coming months.

item.title: What to Know About the Rising Price of Oil and Its Effects on Airline Ticket Prices
item.description: The Gulf conflict has led to higher fuel prices, and the cost of operating a plane has sharply increased. For travelers, that likely means higher fares.
item.dc:creator: Elaine Glusac
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Raghed Waked/Reuters
item.media:description: How much the cost of an airline ticket may rise is unclear. The last time oil rose more than 40 percent — in 2022, on the heels of Russia’s invasion of Ukraine — airfares jumped 28 percent.

item.title: Rising Fuel Costs Pressure Airlines and Truckers
item.description: The price of jet fuel and diesel has surged since the war in Iran began, which could force airlines and trucking companies to pass on higher costs to their customers.
item.dc:creator: Niraj Chokshi, Emmett Lindner and Peter Eavis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: The cost of jet fuel has reached record highs since the U.S.-led war with Iran began.

item.title: Pakistan Sends Navy Escort for Its Ships in the Middle East
item.description: Pakistan, Iran’s neighbor, has been striving to remain neutral in the conflict. But its government and military have been forced to act to protect its economy.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Aamir Qureshi/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Pakistan’s government has announced stringent fuel-saving measures as the conflict in the Middle East chokes oil supplies from the Persian Gulf.

item.title: U.S. Orders Diplomats to Leave Consulate in Southern Turkey
item.description: The order came after the interception of two missiles heading toward Turkey, which has a base that hosts U.S. and NATO forces.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon and Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Sertac Kayar/Reuters
item.media:description: A Turkish soldier stands guard in a field as officials search for debris from the interception of a missile launched toward Turkey.

item.title: Black Rain and Health Fears After Strikes on Iran Fuel Depots
item.description: Health experts warn of long-term respiratory and neurological risks as smoke from burning oil spreads across the region.
item.dc:creator: Emily Baumgaertner Nunn and Parin Behrooz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Late-night airstrikes on oil sites in Tehran still left rolling waves of smoke on Sunday.

item.title: New Supreme Leader Inherits Sprawling, Secretive Office That Dominates Iran
item.description: His father, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, had turned what was traditionally a religious affairs office into a shadowy national security juggernaut.
item.dc:creator: Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Rouzbeh Fouladi/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Mojtaba Khamenei, the son of the former supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, in Tehran in 2019.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay, Liam Stack, Helene Cooper, Eric Schmitt and Euan Ward

item.title: This is what happened on March 9.
Civilians Killed by Strikes in Gulf States Are Almost All Migrant Workers
item.description: Since the American-Israeli attack on Iran began, at least 12 civilians have been killed in oil-rich Gulf countries. All but one of them were foreign nationals.
item.dc:creator: Zia ur-Rehman, Vivian Nereim and Aie Balagtas See
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Christopher Pike/Getty Images
item.media:description: Smoke rising last week after an explosion in an industrial zone of Fujairah, United Arab Emirates.

item.title: Missile in Deadly Iranian School Strike Appears to Be U.S.-Made, Photos Taken by Iran Show
item.description: Iranian state media posted mangled remnants it claims were from the Feb. 28 attack in Minab. An analysis shows they have the markings of a missile made by American manufacturers
item.dc:creator: Christiaan Triebert, Malachy Browne and John Ismay
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Trump Seeks to Calm Oil and Gas Markets but Says Iran War Will Go On
item.description: The president said the U.S. could accompany tankers through the Strait of Hormuz if necessary to keep oil flowing. “We’ve already won in many ways, but we haven’t won enough,” he said.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Benoit Tessier/Reuters
item.media:description: Oil tankers sat anchored in Muscat, Oman, on Saturday as Iran vowed to block the Strait of Hormuz amid the conflict.

item.title: Trump Again Suggests Without Evidence That Iran Struck Elementary School
item.description: After a video showed a Tomahawk missile hitting a naval base next to the school, President Trump claimed Iran possesses such weapons. But the U.S. developed the missile and has sold it only to a few close allies.
item.dc:creator: Shawn McCreesh and John Ismay
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: The president suggested without giving evidence on Monday that Iran or another country could have fired the weapon.

item.title: Shells Fired From Lebanon Land West of Syrian Capital, Country’s Military Says
item.description: The Syrian military accused Hezbollah of targeting Syrian army positions outside of Damascus.
item.dc:creator: Dayana Iwaza and Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Khalil Ashawi/Reuters
item.media:description: President Ahmed al-Shara at a conference last month in Damascus. On Monday, he called the escalation in the Middle East “an existential threat to the entire region.”

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the Conflict on Monday
item.description: President Trump gave conflicting signals about a possible end to the war with Iran amid wild gyrations in global markets.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump speaking from Doral, Fla., on Monday, initially said the war with Iran was “very complete, pretty much.”

item.title: Oil Prices and Stocks Swing Wildly as Iran and Israel Launch New Strikes
item.description: Oil prices and stocks initially swung wildly, and Iran’s foreign minister promised “surprises,” calling the strikes against his country “Operation Epic Mistake.”
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Farnaz Fassihi, Richard Pérez-Peña and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: People in Tehran showing their support on Monday for Mojtaba Khamenei, who was selected as Iran’s new supreme leader.

item.title: Australia Grants Humanitarian Visas to 5 Members of Iranian Women’s Soccer Team
item.description: Concern for the safety of the players had grown after Iranian state media criticized them for not singing the national anthem at a game in Australia.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Kim and Laura Chung
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Dave Hunt/AAPImage, via Associated Press
item.media:description: The Iranian national women’s soccer team played the Philippines in its last match in the Asian Cup tournament in Australia on Sunday.

item.title: Oman Becomes Unlikely Hub for Evacuees Fleeing War in Gulf
item.description: The international airport in Muscat, the Omani capital, has remained open while fighting in the region has escalated.
item.dc:creator: Turki Al-Balushi and Ismaeel Naar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Vadim Ghirda/Associated Press
item.media:description: Passengers from an evacuation flight from Muscat, Oman, at Henri Coanda International Airport, just north of Bucharest, Romania, last week.

item.title: France Is Sending a Large Naval Force to the Middle East
item.description: President Emmanuel Macron said the warships would help protect France’s allies in the region, and could be part of a force to escort ships through the Strait of Hormuz. He said the war could continue for “several days, maybe several weeks.”
item.dc:creator: Catherine Porter and Ana Castelain
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Pool photo by Gonzalo Fuentes
item.media:description: President Emmanuel Macron, center right, visiting the French aircraft carrier Charles de Gaulle during a visit to Cyprus on Monday.

item.title: Iran Choosing Mojtaba Khamenei as Supreme Leader Signals Defiance Amid War
item.description: Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei could prove to be even more radical than his father and predecessor, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed by the U.S. and Israel at the start of the war.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: People gathered in Tehran on Monday, carrying flags and posters of Mojtaba Khamenei, to show support for Iran’s supreme leader.

item.title: At a cemetery in Tehran, there are fresh graves and angry slogans.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Women crying at the burial of a relative who was killed by U.S.-Israeli airstrikes, in Tehran, Iran’s capital.

item.title: Video Captures Apparent Strike Near Boys’ School in Iran
item.description: A communications tower seemed to be the intended target, according to satellite imagery analysis. Iranian state media reported that a boy had been killed in the explosion.
item.dc:creator: Christiaan Triebert and Parin Behrooz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: G7 Countries Hold Off on Releasing Emergency Oil Supplies
item.description: Finance ministers for the seven industrialized countries met on Monday and said they would consider releasing oil from reserves but were not ready to do so now.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott and Aurelien Breeden
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:credit: Reuters
item.media:description: Smoke rose from a refinery operated by Bapco Energies, Bahrain’s state-owned energy company, on Monday. Global oil prices have spiked since the U.S.-led war with Iran began.

item.title: NATO Intercepts Second Iranian Missile Entering Turkey’s Airspace, Defense Ministry Says
item.description: It was the second time in six days that Turkey announced the interception of a missile from Iran.
item.dc:creator: Ben Hubbard
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Michaela Stache/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Mark Rutte, NATO’s secretary general, said the alliance did not need to activate its mutual defense clause.

item.title: Dozens Wounded in Bahrain as Arab States Condemn Iranian Strikes
item.description: Key U.S. allies in the Persian Gulf have stepped up their criticism of Iran after facing a barrage of missiles and drone attacks over the past week.
item.dc:creator: Raja Abdulrahim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Reuters
item.media:description: Smoke rising from a refinery operated by Bapco Energies, Bahrain’s state-owned energy company, on Sitra Island, Bahrain, on Monday.

item.title: Ed Davey Wants King Charles to Cancel his U.S. State Visit Over Iran War
item.description: Ed Davey, the leader of the Liberal Democrats in Britain, said President Trump does not deserve the “diplomatic coup” of hosting the king for a state visit.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
item.media:credit: Andrew Matthews/Press Association, via Associated Press
item.media:description: King Charles III was expected to travel to the United States to meet President Trump next month, as part of celebrations for the 250th anniversary of independence.

item.title: Israeli Forces Raid New Area in Southern Lebanon, Bombard Near Beirut
item.description: Joseph Aoun, the Lebanese president, called for direct talks with Israel to end the fighting. He also leveled blame at Hezbollah, the Iran-backed group, for igniting the war.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman and Christina Goldbaum
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: An airstrike in the Dahiya neighborhood in the southern outskirts of Beirut, in Lebanon, on Monday.
